access_logs.description: Details about the access logs for the load balancer, including bucket name, prefix, and whether logging is enabled.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies the load balancer.

availability_zones.description: The list of availability zones where the load balancer is available.

connection_draining.description: Indicates whether connection draining is enabled for the load balancer.

connection_draining_timeout.description: The time in seconds to allow existing connections to close before deregistering an instance.

cross_zone_load_balancing.description: Indicates whether cross-zone load balancing is enabled.

desync_mitigation_mode.description: The desynchronization mitigation mode for the load balancer.

dns_name.description: The DNS name of the load balancer.

health_check.description: Configuration for health checks performed on the load balancer's instances.

id.description: The ID of the load balancer.

idle_timeout.description: The amount of time, in seconds, that the connection is allowed to be idle.

instances.description: The list of instance IDs that are currently registered with the load balancer.

internal.description: Indicates whether the load balancer is internal or internet-facing.

listener.description: Details about the listeners configured for the load balancer.

security_groups.description: The security groups associated with the load balancer.

source_security_group.description: The security group that you can use as part of your inbound rules for your load balancer's back-end instances.

source_security_group_id.description: The ID of the source security group associated with the load balancer.

subnets.description: The subnets that are attached to the load balancer.

tags.description: A map of tags assigned to the load balancer.

zone_id.description: The canonical hosted zone ID of the load balancer.
